key_insight: The paper argues that LLM-based theorem provers should evolve from solving predefined formal problems to acting as research agents capable of frontier mathematical discovery with rigorous proof support.
engineering_relevance: For computational science and engineering, this reframes AI4Math as infrastructure for hypothesis generation, formal verification, and interactive exploration rather than only proof automation.
method: Reviews the AI4Math pipeline: datasets, auto-formalization, and proof synthesis.
Identifies system gaps across relational structure, exploration, tools, and human-AI collaboration.
Proposes a roadmap for building research-oriented formal mathematics agents.
evidence_signal: The abstract does not report new benchmark results; instead, it synthesizes prior work and diagnoses limitations in current theorem-proving systems.
watch_item: A key implementation concern is that frontier mathematics is open-ended and under-specified, so success will depend on robust tool integration and human-in-the-loop workflows.
paper_title: From Solvers to Research: Large Language Model-Driven Formal Mathematics at the Research Frontier
